directive.title: RFQ detail page loading skeleton and error boundary

auditor.summary: The build implements the core requirements: RFQDetailSkeleton component with proper accessibility attributes, loading.tsx for SSR streaming, error card with retry functionality, and Playwright tests. However, there are critical issues with the ErrorBoundary integration - the existing ErrorBoundary component does not support the render-prop pattern used in the generated code, and it cannot catch errors from async server components. Additionally, TypeScript strict mode violations exist with 'any' type usage. The build needs restructuring to use the correct error handling pattern for Next.js App Router (error.tsx) and proper type definitions before it can pass.

top_issues.description: ErrorBoundary fallback prop type mismatch: The existing ErrorBoundary component expects fallbackTitle?: string as a prop, not a render function. The generated code passes fallback={(error: any) => <RFQErrorCard error={error} />} which is incompatible with the ErrorBoundary interface.

The ErrorBoundary component from components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx does not catch errors thrown from async server components. ErrorBoundary is a client component that catches render errors, but errors thrown from async server components during data fetching are not caught by client-side error boundaries - they need to be handled by error.tsx or caught within the server component itself.
The RFQDetailContent component uses 'any' type for lineItems and auditTrail items, violating TypeScript strict mode requirements.
